Diagrams, Videos & Links

Learn Origami

A curated collection of instruction and inspiration — for folders of every level.

  • Beginner Resources
  • Recommended Books
  • Where to Buy Paper
  • Helpful Videos
Start Here

Beginner Resources

Never folded before? These are the best places to start — free, clear, and beginner-friendly.

Origami Resource Center

Step-by-step diagrams for beginners through advanced. One of the most comprehensive free collections online.

Origami Instructions

Clear photo-based instructions for hundreds of models. Great for beginners who prefer pictures over diagrams.

Origami Fun

Simple, illustrated diagrams for kids and beginners. A friendly starting point with classic models.

OrigamiUSA Free Diagrams

A curated library of free diagrams from OrigamiUSA — vetted, quality-checked, and organized by difficulty.

Gilad's Origami Page

An encyclopedic database of origami books, designers, and models. The definitive reference for serious folders.

Robert J. Lang

Website of one of the foremost origami artists and theorists in the world. Includes TreeMaker software.
Member Recommendations

Recommended Books

These titles have inspired and educated our members over the years — including works by our own founders.

Beginner

Florence Temko
Origami for Beginners

One of many beloved books by our co-founder, making origami accessible to all ages.

Advanced

Robert J. Lang
Origami Design Secrets

The definitive book on the mathematical principles behind designing original origami models.

Philosophy

Peter Engel
Origami from Angelfish to Zen

A beautiful exploration of the philosophy, art, and craft of paper folding.

Geometric

Eric Gjerde
Origami Tessellations

The go-to guide for tessellation folding — geometric patterns that create stunning surfaces.

Classic

Kunihiko Kasahara
Origami Omnibus

A comprehensive guide covering hundreds of models from simple to complex by one of Japan's most celebrated folders.

Modular

Tomoko Fuse
Unit Origami

The essential reference for modular origami — creating complex 3D structures from multiple folded units.

Animals

John Montroll
Animal Origami for the Enthusiast

Detailed diagrams for realistic animal models. A classic in the origami canon.

Mathematics

Perla Myers & Arnold Tubis
Origami and Math

Exploring the deep connection between paper folding and geometry — by two of our own.

Stock Up

Where to Buy Paper

The right paper makes all the difference. Here's where our members shop.

Origami Shop (US)

Wide selection of kami, washi, and specialty papers. Good range for all skill levels.

Amazon

Convenient for bulk kami packs. Look for Toyo or Daiso brand for reliable quality at a low price.

Hobby Lobby

Carry basic origami paper packs in-store. Good for picking up supplies locally in San Diego.

Michaels

Widely available in San Diego. Good for standard kami and decorative papers.

Jansson Origami

Specialty papers including washi, foil, and tissue-foil for advanced folders.

In a Pinch

Any square piece of paper works for practice. Cut a sheet of printer paper into a square and you're ready to fold.

Watch & Learn

Helpful Videos

Sometimes seeing it fold in real time makes all the difference. These channels are the best on YouTube.

Tadashi Mori

High-quality tutorials from simple to complex. Exceptional production and clear step-by-step instruction.

Origami Instructions

Hundreds of models from easy to complex. A reliable channel for following along step by step.

Jo Nakashima

Beautiful and creative models, many original designs. Great for intermediate to advanced folders.

Origami Tree

Clear beginner-friendly tutorials with a focus on fun, approachable models. Great for new folders.

Tobias Zimmermann

Complex modular and geometric origami. For advanced folders looking for a serious challenge.

Search YouTube

Thousands of origami tutorials exist on YouTube. Search any model name + "origami tutorial" to find step-by-step guides.
GSDOG

Greater San Diego Origami Group
San Diego, California
